title: SIAHA DISTRICT-A POL LEH LPG DINHMUN SAWIHO A NI, MIPUI TAN THLAPHANNA TUR A AWM LO
content:
5003/2025-2026

Dated Siaha, the 25 March, 2026:Vawiin chawhma dar 11:00A.M. khan Pu VL Hruaizela Khiangte, District Bawrhsap hovin a office chamber-ah Siaha district-a tuialhthei (Petroleum, Oil & Lubricants) leh eirawngbawlna gas (Liquefied Petroleum Gas) dinhmun sawiho a ni a, Siaha district Joint control room member-te an tel, Joint Control Room member-te chu District Administrator, Police, DCSO leh DIPRO official-te an ni.

Pu VL Hruaizela Khiangte chuan March 20, 2026-ah Tuialhthei lei leh hralh chungchangah thupek chhuah anih tawh thu sawiin, “Siaha district tana lo thleng tuialhthei leh eirawngbawlna gas hi hmun dang leh ram dangah a chhuah kan remti lo, ram chhunga cheng mipui (consumer) te hi dah pawimawh hmasak ber tur a ni, a ti. Kawnga thang mek pawh a thlenna tur hmun thleng lova hruaikawi leh khawih chingpen a awm loh nan chak taka hma lak anih thu a sawi bawk.

Bawrhsap chuan mipuite chu thuneitute thu ngaichang thiam tura chahin, ‘thupek ṭha taka zawm anih hian mipui te’n rualkhai takin kan ni tin mamawh tawk ṭheuh kan chang thei a, chutiang ni lova mahni duh zat zat kan lei chuan mi dangte tan harsatna kan siam thei a ni, a ti. Mipuite chu um leh thawl (gallon) hmanga tuialhthei la lo turin a chah a, a thawkte pawh chutianga lak phal lo turin a hrilh bawk.

Joint control room meeting chuan Siaha district chhungah dik leh felfai taka tuialhthei leh eirawngbawlna gas lei leh hralh anih theih nan Filling station leh Gas Agent-ah te Closed-Circuit Television (CCTV) vuah ni se, District Administrator, SP leh DCSO-in vil se tih an rel a, tun dinhmunah ni tin mamawh a thleng ṭhaa district chhunga cheng mipuite tan tuialhthei leh eirawngbawlna gas-ah thlaphanna tur a awm lo.

March ni 25, 2026 (dar 10:00AM) a Siaha district tuialhthei (POL) dinhmun chu:-

1.J. Rama filling Station, Siaha: MS 9000, HSD 7000 (kawnga thang mêk: HSD trip khat, MS trip 1)

2.SPC filling station, Tipa V: MS 19000, HSD 18500 (kawnga thang mêk: HSD: Nil, MS: Nil)

3.Immanuel filling Station, Siaha: MS 11000, HSD 13000 (kawnga thang mêk: HSD: trip 1, MS: Nil)

March ni 25, 2026 (dar 10:00AM) a Siaha district eirawngbawlna gas (LPG) dinhmun chu:-

1.Mizofed, Siaha: Domestic (Kg 14.2) bur 324 niin, kawnga thang mêk bur 1026 (trip 3) a ni.

2.Mizofed, Siaha: Commerical ah bur 18

Siaha district-a tuialhthei (POL) leh eirawngbawlna gas (LPG) chungchanga zualko duh emaw thu puarpawleng kara thu chiang zawk hre duh emaw tan phone number tarlanah hian Joint control room member-te an biak theih e: 6909538406, 6909716830

title: LAWNGTLAI DISTRICT LEVEL WORKSHOP ON HANDHOLDING SCHEME NEIH A NI
content:
5002/2025-2026

Lawngtlai, the 25th March, 2026 :Vawiin chawhma dar 11:00 AM khan Deputy Commissioner, Conference Hall, Lawngtlai ah District Level Workshop on Handholding Scheme neih niin Dr.Lorrain Lalpekliana Chinzah, MLA & Adviser to Chief Minister chu khuallian niin a hmanpui.

Khuallian Dr.Lorrain Lalpekliana Chinzah,Adviser to Chief  Minister chuan 'District Level Workshop on Handholding Scheme Lawngtlai District' hawnna neiin workshop an nei thei chu lawmawm a tih thu sawiin workshop hmang tura kal zawngte chibai a buk a ni. Lawngtlai District-  a Line Department- te thahnem an ngaiin  an hah hle a, Sokar theih tawp an chhuahpuinaah an chhuanawm hle ani a ti a, an chungah lawmthu a sawi a ni. He Hand Holding Scheme hi Miharsa zawk ten lei rem an rah theihna, miharsa zawk tih tak tak tum, eizawnna duh tan Sorkar Scheme kalpui mek a ni, a ti. Mizoram Sorkar a Line department te hmalakna ah lawmawm hle  a ti a, chutih rualin Lawngtlai District bik hi district hnufual kan ni a, vawiin ni a kan mi leh sa te Hand Holding Scheme kaltlang a an dinhmun kan siam a ngai tiin chumi atan chuan Lawngtlai District hi kan tih chak a ngai ani, a ti. MLA chuan Handholding Scheme  hlawhtlinna tur atana a pawimawh em em chu Bank te an ni a, line department in felfai takin ruahmanna siam mahse, Bank in an pawmpui  tlat loh chuan tih ngaihna awm lo a ni a ti. Bank lam pawh tan la zual a Handholding Scheme kaltlanga loan dilte ngaipawimawh turin a chah a ni. Dr. Lorrain Lalpekliana Chinzah chuan Handholding Scheme hmanga miharsa zawk ten lei rem an rah ngei theih nan Line department leh Bank lam te theih tawp chhuah tlang zel turin a ngen in a sawm bawk a ni.

Pu Donny Lalruatsanga, Lawngtlai Bawrhsap chuan vawiin ni a 'District Level Workshop on Handholding Scheme' neih theih a ni chu a lawmwm hle a, Stake holder kal lkhawmte hnenah lawmthu a sawi a. Bawrhsap chuan Handholding Scheme hi miharsa zawk ten leirem an rah ve theihna tur a sokar ruahman a ni a,  bul tan thar duh tan leh bul tan tawh sa tan pawh bank loan kaltlang a inkhaichhuahna scheme a ni a, Sorkar hmala na êng ah lei rem kan rap chho dawn a ni, a ti. Handholding hi bul tan chauh mah nise, a hlawh na teltu tam tak kan awm a, kan district ah pawh hmalaknna a kal mek a ni a ti. Kan thlen chinah hian hma kan sawn mek zel a, tun platform ah hian a tak tak a ti hlawhtlingtu chu bank te kan ni a, department in ruahmanna te a siam a, an lo recomend te bank atangin verification te neiin  dawng tlak a an ngaih te pek an ni a, Bank lam hi a hnarkaitu tak tak an nih avangin tan la ila, diltu te dawng thei tur a buaipui leh buatsaih hi kan tih tur a ni a ti bawk. Application fello-  te pawh tih felpui dan ngaihtuah hram hram turin kan ngaihtuahna kan sen a ngai a ni,  a ti. Lawngtlai District - ah handholding scheme kaltlang a hlawhtlingna chang turin Sorkar, Line Department leh Bank lamte  hruihrual pawh mar tha turin hruihrual kan pawh tlan vek a ngai a ni a ti.

He hunah hian Pu H Lallianzuala, MSPCC Member chuan The Mizoram Bana Kaih Scheme 2024 sawifiahna neiin CM Special Pakage Phase-II ah Lawngtlai District bikah 411 Screened, 6 approved niin fund released a awm lo a, Loan Component bikah Phase-II ah dilna submit-10 niin submitted stage-6 a nih thu ngaihthlak a ni. Loan Component (Disbursed Banks) dt.10/05/2026 thleng a Mizoram pumpui ah hetiang hi  pek tawh dan- SBI - Progress Partners-88, Loan Amounts Rs.8,77,34,912,MRB - Progress Partners-272, Loan Amounts Rs.20,68,87,150

MCAB - Progress Partners-44, Loan Amounts Rs.6,02,42,866 tarlan niin, Hanholding Scheme ah Bank Loan component bikah hian kum 18-60 inkar tan dil theih a ni a, cibil Score 700 aia hniam lo tur niin Ration Card pakhat a hming lang zingah pakhat chauh in  dil theih niin englai pawhin a dil theih reng a nih thu ngaihthlak a ni. CM Special Pakage bik ah hian Project in a mamawh dan a zirin Rs.50,000 thleng dil theih anih thu ngaihtlak a ni bawk. Line Department leh Society hruaitute fuihna thu sawiin Loan bikah Scheme thlang thiam turin a chah nghal bawk ani.

Pu Lalhruaizela, SDC in District Monitoring Committee aiawhin Lawngtlai District report pe in tun dinhmunah Loan Component ah Horticulture - Loan Sanction -2, LRSWC Loan Sanction - 1, DVO - Loan Sanction-1, DCIO - Loan Sanction-4, Fisheries - Loan Sanction-1, Sericulture - NIL, Agriculture - Loan Sanction-1 a nih thu ngaihthlakna ni. Pu Soren Lead District Manager, Lawngtlai pawhin Bank Loan chungchang report tawi a pe bawk.

He Workshop hi Pu H. Lallianzuala, MSPCC Member,Pu F.Lalrinenga, MSPCC Member, Pu Nengthianlala, PS to CM (Handholding) ten telpuiin zawhna leh chhanna hun hman a ni a. KCC chungchang inhrilhhriatna NABARD lam atanga ngaihthlak a ni a, 'Demonstation of application Process of Handholding' Screen Play neih niin, DIPRO Office Lawngtlai in Banakaih a hlawhtlingte 'Success Story' video entirna an nei bawk. Dr.Lalmuanpuia Samte, DVO, Lawngtlai in lawmthu sawina hun a hmang a ni.

Vawiin Workshop ah hian Lawngtlai District chhunga Line Department, Bank leh Society hruaitute an tel kim hle a ni.

title: Lungleiah Bana Kaih Phase–II hnuaia CM Special Package progress partner training leh certificate inhlanna buatsaih a ni
content:
5001/2025-2026

Lunglei, 2026 Vau thla ni 25:Vawiin khan Lunglei District Bawrhsap Pi Navneet Mann hovin a hna thawh pindanah Mizoram sawrkar flagship programme, Mizoram Bana Kaih (Handholding) Scheme Phase–II hnuaia Lunglei District-a Chief Minister's Special Package ṭanpuina dawng tur progress partner tana training leh certificate inhlanna hun buatsaih a ni.

Bana Kaih Phase–II hnuaiah hian Lunglei District-ah CM Special Package-ah progress partner thlan fel tawh pakhat awmin, Pu Lalnunmawia, Ṭawipui South, Ar vulh (Poultry Farming)-a hma la tur a ni a. Vawiin khan DC Office-a official-te leh AH & Veterinary Department Southern Zone Joint Director Dr Ngurzidingi Sailo te hovin progress partner tana hriat tur pawimawh hrang hrang inhrilhfiahna leh certificate inhlanna hi buatsaih a ni.

District Bawrhsap Pi Navneet Mann chuan Bana Kaih Scheme chu Mizoram sawrkar flagship scheme a nih angin department-te leh progress partner-te pawhin tih tak takna nena kalpui a ṭul thu sawiin, a tihhlawhtlin kawngah ṭha taka thawh ho leh ṭan rual a pawimawh thu a sawi a. Phase–II dilna hawn mek chu Lunglei District mipuite'n ngaihvenin, a tam thei ang berin dil se a duh thu sawiin, dilna lut aṭangin hma la tak tak turte chu CM Special Package leh Loan Component-ah progress partner atan la thlan belh zel tur an nih thu a sawi.

Official-te chuan progress partner tana hriat tur pawimawh hrang hrang sawiin, an hma lakna kawngah department mi thiamte rawn hreh lo tur leh hlawhtling ngei tura hma lak tum turin an chah a. An hma lakna chu a theih ang anga an vil zui tur thu an sawi.

Progress Partner hnenah hian Certificate leh Placard hlan a ni a. Progress Partner Pu Lalnunmawia chuan ar a vulh chhoh dan leh hma lak zel a tum dan sawiin, progress partner-a thlan a nih avanga a lawmzia leh theih tawpa hma lak a tum thu a sawi bawk.

Bana Kaih CM Special Package ṭanpuina hi ₹50,000/- niin, progress partner-te bank account-ah Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT)-a thun tura ruahman a ni a. Certificate inhlan fel hnuah hian bank kal tlangin inpek fel nghal a ni ang.

Attachment

title: Mizoram Governor Inaugurates One Day Skills Development Conclave in Aizawl
content:
559/2025-2026

Aizawl, the 25th March, 2026 :Mizoram Governor, General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh, PVSM, AVSM, YSM (Retd), today graced the inaugural programme of the "One Day Skills Development Conclave" at the ICFAI University Mizoram campus in Durtlang North, Aizawl, as the Chief Guest. Prof. Y. R. Haragopal Reddy, Chancellor of ICFAI University Mizoram, attended the event as the Guest of Honour. The conclave was jointly organised by ICFAI University Mizoram and the Department of Labour, Employment, Skills Development & Entrepreneurship, Govt. of Mizoram.

In his inaugural address, Governor General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh praised the talented and hardworking nature of Mizo youth. He emphasised that, in addition to their inherent abilities, they must acquire the right skills, greater exposure, and confidence to compete not only at the national level but also on the global stage. While urging them to seize opportunities across India and the world, he advised the youth to remain firmly rooted in the rich values and cultural heritage of Mizoram, noting that this foundation would propel them to even greater heights of success. Highlighting Mizoram’s abundant natural beauty, vibrant culture, and peaceful social environment, the Governor pointed out the immense potential for the state to excel in the service sector - currently the fastest-growing sector in the economy. He stressed that skill development must go hand in hand with innovation and entrepreneurship, and expressed hope that the conclave would prove highly beneficial for the youth. He added that the event should not end merely as a platform for discussion, but evolve into a dynamic movement that inspires and transforms young people, converting their skills into real opportunities and guiding them towards success in life.

The Governor described the youth as a priceless asset to both the state and the nation, and encouraged them not to limit their ambitions to Mizoram alone. Instead, they should aim for excellence across India and the world. He underscored the urgent need to create the right direction and ecosystem for skill development and entrepreneurship, declaring that “skills are the true currency of success in the modern era.” Noting that India leads the world with the highest number of startups launched daily, he called upon the youth to focus on practical knowledge, adaptability, and continuous learning - qualities that are in high demand in today’s job market. Since the government alone cannot provide employment for everyone, he urged greater emphasis on the private sector and entrepreneurship. He encouraged young people to aspire not just to be job seekers, but to become job creators. The Governor further stressed the importance of integrating practical skills with academic learning, forging stronger linkages between educational institutions, industries, and markets, nurturing entrepreneurial thinking among the youth, and promoting close collaboration among all stakeholders to achieve meaningful success in skill development initiatives.

Prof. (Dr.) Ginlianlal Buhril, Vice Chancellor of ICFAI University Mizoram, delivered the welcome address. Insightful speeches were also given by Prof. YR Haragopal Reddy, Chancellor, and Pu John Tanpuia, Director of the LESDE Department. Dr. David Lawmzuala, Registrar of ICFAI University Mizoram, proposed the vote of thanks and formally concluded the inaugural session.

Following the inauguration, three distinguished resource persons shared their expertise: Major Dr. Gulshan Sharma, Director General of Medical Wellness & Spiritual Tourism and Strategic Consultant for MSMEs in the Service Industry, Gujarat; Shri Pinaki Roychoudhury, Social Entrepreneur and Group CEO from Kolkata and Shri K. Yatish Rajawat, CEO of the Center for Innovation in Public Policy and Founding Partner of the Hundred Million Jobs Foundation. They delivered sessions on two key themes - “From Mizoram to the World” and “Mizo Youths Reaching Out to Pan India” - followed by a highly engaging and productive panel discussion.

Attachment

title: Mizoram Larsap-in One Day Skills Development Conclave a hawng
content:
5000/2025-2026

Aizawl, the 25th March, 2026:ICFAI University Mizoram leh Labour, Employment, Skills Development & Entrepreneurship Department, Govt. of Mizoram buatsaih, "One Day Skills Development Conclave", ICFAI University Mizoram Campus, Durtlang North, Aizawla neih chu vawiin khan Larsap General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh, PVSM, AVSM, YSM (Retd) chuan Khuallian niin a hawng. He hunah hian Prof. YR Haragopal Reddy, Chancellor, ICFAI University Mizoram chu Khualzahawm a ni.

Larsap General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh chuan Mizo thalaite chu pianpui thiamna nei tha leh thawkrim an nih thu sawiin, India leh khawvel pum inelnaa lang pha turin thiamna dik tak, inphochhuahna hun tha leh inrintawkna neih chu tun dinhmuna an mamawh a ni, a ti a. Thalaite chu India leh khawvel huap ang pawha inphochhuahna hun remchang an lo hmu anih pawhin, mahni hnam ziarang leh chawr chhuahna hlamchhiah lova nghet taka vuan zel turin a chah a; hetiang tih nachang in hriat phawt chuan hlawhtlinna lian zawkin a umzui ang che u, a ti a. Ram leilung mawi, hnam ziarang hausa leh ralmuang anih avangtein, Mizoramin tunlaia sector thang duang ber service sector hnuaiah hlawkna a tel thu a sawi a. Kutthemthiamna tipung turin chin dan thar leh mahni kutkawih eizawnna kalkawp tir thiam a tul tih sawiin, skills conclave chu sawi honaah tawp mai lo se, hmalam panna, kawng dik inkawhhmuhna, infuihtharna leh danglamna thlentuah tang se a duh thu a sawi bawk.

Larsap chuan thalaite chu state leh rampum tana rohlu leh innghahna an nih thu sawiin, khawtualah lungawi mai lova, India mai nilo khawvel hmun dang thlenga hlawhtlinna chan tum turin a chah a. Skill development leh entrepreneurship chawilar turin kawng dik leh boruak tha siam a tul tih sawiin, thiamna chu hlawhtling tura pawimawh a ni, a ti a. India chu khawvela nikhata startup din tam ber anih mek thu a sawi bawk a. Sawrkar hnain mi zawng zawng a daih sen loh avangin private sector leh entrepreneurship lam ngaihven deuh deuh a tul tih sawiin, thalaite chu hna zawngtu ni lova siam saktu nih tum turin a fuih bawk.

He hunah hian Prof (Dr.) Ginlianlal Buhril, Vice Chancellor, ICFAI University Mizoram chuan kalkhawmte lawmna thu a sawi a. Prof. YR Haragopal Reddy, Chancellor, ICFAI University Mizoram leh Pu John Tanpuia, Director, LESDE Department-te hnen atangin thusawi ngaihthlak a ni a. Dr. David Lawmzuala, Registrar, ICFAI University Mizoram chuan lawmthu sawiin hawnna hun a khar a ni.

Hemi hnu hian resource person atana sawm mithiam 3 - Major. Dr. Gulshan Sharma, Director General Medical Wellness & Spiritual Tourism, Strategic Consutant, MSMEs for Service Industry, Gujarat; Pu Pinaki Roychoudhury, Social Entrepreneur, Group CEO, Kolkata leh Pu K. Yatish Rajawat, CEO, Center For Innovation in Public Policy and Founding Partner, Hundred Million Jobs Foundation te'n topic thlan bik pahnih "From Mizoram to the world" leh "Mizo Youths reaching out to Pan India", tih thupui hmangtein zirtirna an pe a, sawi hona hlawk thlak tak an hmang a ni.

Attachment

content:
558/2025-2026

Vanlalvena, Superintendent of Police, Traffic, Aizawl chuan a hnuaia mi ang hian thupek a chhuah.

title: Serchhip DC hovin Reserved Forest chungchang sawiho
content:
4998/2025-2026

Dated Serchhip, the 25th March, 2026:Vawiin khan Serchhip DC Meeting Roomah Reserved Forest chungchang sawiin District Level Committee chu DC Pu Paul L Khuma hoin an thukhawm.

Supreme Court thupek angin Land Revenue & Settlement Department chuan contour-based mapping of District area -wise Reserved Forest chu MIRSAC Director hma la turin a hriattir tawh a. District tin Assistant Commissioner (Revenue) te chu an District huam chhung theuha Reserved Forest huam chhunga mimal ram nei te list lo peihfel tura tih an ni bawk a. Ram relocate ngai te, landholding cancel tul te leh harsatna awm apiangte sawiho turin March 2026 chawlhkar thumna ah SIT, GAD Meeting neih a ni ang. Council of Minister pawmpuina chu April 2026 chhunga lak tur niin, Supreme Court thupeka hmalakna Affidavit/report file chu May 2026 chhungin tihfel tur a ni bawk.

Supreme court order angin forest reserve area-a ram nei te chu an enkawl dan a zira category siamfel a ngai a, a hmuna endik field verification kalpui vek tur niin April ni 15 2026 hmaa inpeih fel tura beisei an ni. Forest Department chuan verification an lo buaipui anga, VC te pawhin an huam chhunga mi te ngun taka lo enfel turin chah an ni bawk.

He meeting ah hian Additional DC Pu Lalrinzuala, SDO (Sadar) Pi Rebecca Laldinmawii Hrahsel, Assistant Commissioner (Revenue) Pu Gaston Vanlalhriatpuia, SDC Pi C.Vanlalchhanhimi, DFO Thenzawl Dr R.Lalnunrenga, DFO N.Vanlaiphai Pu Thanliana Hauhnar te bakah Thenzawl West, Neihloh, Hualtu leh Hmuntha VC aiawh te an tel a ni.

Attachment

title: MINISTER PU C. LALSAWIVUNGA'N LEITHUM HAW RUNPUI HMANPUI
content:
4997/2025-2026

Champhai, dated 25th August 2026:Vawiin khan Pu C. Lalsawivunga, Minister, Art & Culture Department chuan Leithum Haw Runpui chu Khuallian niin a hmanpui a, Pu Ramthanmawia, MLA East Tuipui chu Khual zahawm niin Pu C. Lalthianghlima, Chairman Organising, Committee in hun a khaihruai a, he hunah hian Leithum Khua ti hmingthatute chawmimawina hun hman a ni.

Khuallian Pu C. Lalsawivunga chuan Leithum khua hian sakhuanaah, zirnaah, sawrkar leh ram rorelna thlengin mi chhuanawm tak tak a hring chhuak tawh tih a sawi a. Khua leh tui tha nih hi thil hlu leh pawimawh tak a ni a, an khaw mi leh sa te chu awmna hmun theuhah intodelh duhna thinlung pu a, thahnemngai taka mahni hma zawnah rinawmna vawng tlat a, an eizawnna kawng theuhah hma sawna rahbi rap tur leh intodelh khua ni a, khaw dangte entawn turin rinawmna nen hma la turin a chah a. Haw Runpui hi in unauna, in hmangaihna ti pungtu ni tur leh thlarau leh taksa lamah hmasawnna thlentu ni a, hlim taka hmang turin duhsakna a hlan a ni.

Khual Zahawm Pu Ramthanmawia, MLA, East Tupui pawhin thusawiin Khawtlang hmasawnna tura zirna kawnga tan lak a tul zia te, Ramngaw leh tuihna humhalh tul zia te, Ruihhlo in kan ram leh hnam a tih chhiat nasat zia leh khawtlang tangruala do a tul tawh zia te, dik taka sum leh pai lak luh a that thu leh sum leh pai lak luh ang ang te khawl nachang hriat a that zia te sawiin Haw Runpui hmang tura kal khawmte a chah bawk.

Leithum khua hi khaw hlun tak niin Hualngo Lal Hualzasela (Pa Huala) leh a thuihhruai ten kum 1896 khan an luh tan a, kum 1996 khan Centenary lawm tawhin tunah hian kum 130 na an hmang mek a ni.

Attachment

title: DAPCC CHAIRMAN KAIHHRUAIIN DAPCC THUKHAWM
content:
4996/2025-2026

Dated Aizawl, the 25th March, 2026:Vawiin khan District AIDS Prevention and Control Committee (DAPCC), Aizawl district chuan Aizawl DC Er. Lalhriatpuia, DAPCC Chairman ni bawk kaihhruaina hnuaiah DC conference hall-ah meeting an nei.

DAPCC Chairman chuan Mizoram hi India ram pumpuia HIV hluar berna state a ni chu pawi a tih thu sawiin, Mizoram chuan hmasawnna tur tam tak a la neih thu a sawi a. Chairman chuan HIV darh tur ven kawngah te, HIV test pawimawhzia leh HIV veiten tha taka treatment an lak a pawimawh zia nasa zawka inzirtirna campaign neih a tul thu a sawi a. DAPCC member kalkhawmte chu HIV do kawnga chak taka hma la zel turin a chah a ni.

Dr. R. Zonunmawii, District Programme Manager, DISHA, Aizawl District chuan Aizawl district chhungah puitiling zinga HIV vei awm zat hi 4.049% a ni tih tarlangin kum 15 - 49 inkarah za zelah mi pali (4) ang vel HIV hrik pai anga ngaih an ni. Kum 2025 chhunga Intensified Information, Education & Communication (IIEC) campaign-a an hmalaknate report a pe a. Kum 2025 October leh November chhung khan thingtlang khua 26 an thlanchhuah zinga mi 6504 hnenah leh an khaw hruaitute hnenah HIV chungchang chiang zawka inzirtirna campaign neih niin, HIV leh Syphilis test-te pawh tihsak an ni.

Vawiiin meeting-ah hian Mission AIDS Suraksha chungchang sawi ho niin Mission AIDS Suraksha GOAL 95:95:99 achieve theih nana hmalak dan turte sawi ho a ni a. 95:95:99 awmzia leh thil tum - People Living with HIV (PLHIV) 95% talin an nihna an inhriat theihna tura zau zawka test neih te; HIV hrik pai 95% ten Antiretroviral Therapy (ART) tha taka an lak te; ART latu 99% ten an thatpui a, midang kai chhawng thei lo khawpa viral load hniam an neih dan turte sawi ho a ni. Mission AIDS Suraksha-ah hian Aizawl District dinhmun thlir ho a ni a, tun dinhmunah Aizawl district-a PHLIV 94.5 % te’n an nihna an inhria a, 88.5% te’n ART la mekin, 98.3% te’n midang kai chhawng thei lo turin an thatpui a ni.

HIV darh zau zel tur venna kawngah chak zawk leh tha zawka Aizawl District-in hmasawnna a hmuh theihna turin Integrated Action Plan (IAP) for Aizawl District chu sawi ho a ni a. IAP hnuaia hmalakna tur thenkhatte  sawi ho niin mi tam zawkin HIV an kai leh kai loh an in test theihna turin Awareness campaign nasa zawka neih ni se tih te,  High Risk Group (HRG) te zingah HIV darh zel tur tha lehzuala ven theihna tura hma lak te, HIV kai te’n rang taka an inhriatchhuah theihna tura hma lak te, PHLIV te’n rang tak leh tha taka treatment an neih theihna tura hmalak te, a huhova khawsakna hmun Jail/Home-ahte uar lehzuala awareness kalpui dan tur te, YMA-te nena tha lehzuala thawh hona neih te, ARTC hlata awm, thingtlang leh hmun kilkhawr zawka awmte tan HIV test leh Counseling services awlsam zawka a awm theih nana lirthei Mobile ICTC leh Bikers Outreach Workers Services tih changtlung dan tur te, Social Benefit Schemes hrang hrang leh eizawnna tha zawk an hmuh theih dan turte rel a. DAPCC member kalkhawmte atangin rawtna hrang hrang leh hmalak zel dan turte ngaihthlak a ni bawk.

Attachment

title: Khawzawl District AIDS Prevention Control Committee thukhawm
content:
4995/2025-2026

Dated Khawzawl, 25th March, 2026:Tukin zing dar 9:30 khan DC Chamber-ah District AIDS Prevention Control Committee (DAPCC) chu an Chairman Pu K. Vanlalthiana, Bawrhsap ni bawk hovin an thukhawm.

He meetingah hian District AIDS Prevention Control Committee (DAPCC) member restructuring chungchangte sawiho a ni a, Mission AIDS Suraksha (95:95:95)  meeting lo neih tawh chu thlir ho a ni bawk. DAPCC hmalakna tur hrang hrangte chhawpchhuah niin HIV/AIDS chungchang Awareness campaign Khawzawl District chhunga Kohhran hrang hrangah neih nise, Tin, Kohhran hrang hrangah HIV/AIDS Awareness chungchang Chawlhni zana chhiar chhuah tur DAPCC hminga siam nise tih a ni bawk. Kohhran hrang hrangte an mahni Kohhran theuhah Testing neih dan tur ruahman dan turte HIV/AIDS chungchangah Awareness neih chhunzawm dan turte leh Khawzawl District chhunga Kohhran (Outside Town) Pastor Bial thut hmunah te neih dan turte sawiho a ni bawk a ni.

Attachment

title: MIZORAM GOVERNOR GRACES TLANGRO : THE MIZORAM SILK RUNWAY EVENT IN AIZAWL
content:
Aizawl, the 24th March 24, 2026:Mizoram Governor General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh, PVSM, AVSM, YSM (Retd), graced the grand fashion show titled "Tlangro: The Mizoram Silk Runway" on Tuesday evening at Aijal Club Park in Aizawl. The event, jointly organised by the Development Commissioner (Handlooms), Ministry of Textiles, Government of India, the Sericulture Department, Government of Mizoram and the National Handloom Development Corporation (NHDC), aimed to showcase Mizoram’s rich silk heritage while celebrating the artistry, craftsmanship, and immense untapped potential of the state’s silk industry.

The First Lady, Mrs Bharti Singh, also attended the programme. Other dignitaries present included Chief Secretary Mr. Khilli Ram Meena and Dr. Naresh Babu N, Joint Secretary (Technical) & CEO of the Silk Mark Organisation of India (SMOI), Central Silk Board, who attended as Guests of Honour.

In his address, Governor General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh described sericulture as a sector with huge economic potential that remains largely untapped. He highlighted that Mizoram is capable of producing all four major varieties of silk - mulberry, eri, muga, and oak tasar - and has the natural advantages to emerge as the “Capital of All Kinds of Silk” in the country. He noted that the state’s climate and environment are highly suitable for silkworm rearing. With growing global demand for eco-friendly and sustainable textiles, Mizoram’s natural fibre, particularly eri silk, holds strong potential to capture significant international markets. The Governor emphasised that sericulture aligns perfectly with rural development and women’s empowerment, serving as an ideal supplementary income source that complements traditional agricultural activities. “Sericulture can become a powerful pathway for economic empowerment, cultural preservation, and sustainable development,” he said, while stressing the need for strong collaboration among farmers, government agencies, researchers, and local communities to transform the sector into a genuine engine of growth for the state.

The Governor outlined several key measures required to advance sericulture, including improving awareness and training programmes, ensuring the availability of quality inputs, adopting modern technology, strengthening infrastructure and market linkages, promoting research and development, encouraging greater youth participation, and ensuring effective government support and policy implementation. He also called for sustainability to remain at the core through environmentally friendly practices. Referring to muga silk - a golden-hued variety traditionally associated with Northeast India -  the Governor expressed confidence that Mizoram could take a leading role in its expansion. He suggested strategies such as increasing host plant cultivation, developing a distinct regional brand identity, enhancing skill development in rearing techniques, promoting value-added products, integrating silk with tourism, leveraging digital platforms and e-commerce, collaborating with fashion designers and the broader industry, and securing robust government and institutional backing.

Earlier, Mrs Florence Zotluangpuii, Secretary of the Sericulture Department, delivered the welcome address and expressed gratitude to all participants and partners. The highlight of the evening was a stunning display of exquisite silk garments and creations by fashion designers Tluangpuii Hmar and Violeta by Sangzuali. Insightful addresses were also delivered by Chief Secretary Pu Khilli Ram Meena and Dr. Naresh Babu N, Joint Secretary (Technical) & CEO of the Silk Mark Organisation of India (SMOI), Central Silk Board. The programme concluded with a vote of thanks proposed by Mr. Lalrinawma, Director of the Sericulture Department.
